title: How to Make WinRAR Start Faster on Linux Ubuntu
intro: If WinRAR takes forever to launch, here is a quick fix that actually works.
steps: Open WinRAR and click the gear icon in top right
Go to Settings > Advanced > Startup Behavior
Uncheck "Load on system startup"
Disable any splash screen animations
Restart the app — you should see a noticeable speed improvement
conclusion: Simple fix but makes a huge difference day to day.

title: Cut VS Code Memory Usage from 800MB to 400MB
intro: VS Code was eating up almost 800MB of RAM on my machine. Here is what I changed.
steps: Press Ctrl+Shift+Esc to open Task Manager
In the app, go to Tools > Options > Performance
Turn off hardware acceleration
Reduce undo history from 100 to 20 levels
Disable auto-save for non-critical files
Restart — usage should drop to about 400MB
conclusion: Took me way too long to figure this out. Hope it saves you the headache.

title: How to Recover Unsaved Work in {sw} After a Crash
intro: Power went out while you were working? Do not panic. Your work might be recoverable.
steps: Reopen Inkscape — look for an Auto-Recovery panel on the welcome screen
Go to File > Info > Manage Versions > Recover Unsaved Documents
Navigate to the auto-save folder: %TEMP%/Inkscape/AutoSave
Sort by date modified — your most recent autosave should be at the top
Open the file and immediately Save As to a safe location
conclusion: Bookmark this page. You will need it someday.

title: Everything Will Not Open? Try This Before Reinstalling
intro: Before you waste time reinstalling Everything, try this. It fixes about 80% of launch failures.
steps: Kill any hung Everything processes via Task Manager
Navigate to %APPDATA%/Everything and delete the Cache folder
Rename Preferences.json to Preferences.json.bak to reset settings
Try launching again — if it works, old preferences were corrupted
If it still fails, run Everything as administrator once to fix permission issues
conclusion: Works on {os_name} and should work on earlier versions too.

title: Krita Will Not Open? Try This Before Reinstalling
intro: Before you waste time reinstalling Krita, try this. It fixes about 80% of launch failures.

Navigate to %APPDATA%/Krita and delete the Cache folder

title: Cut Firefox Memory Usage from 200MB to 50MB
intro: Firefox was eating up almost 200MB of RAM on my machine. Here is what I changed.
steps: Press Ctrl+Shift+Esc to open Task Manager
In the app, go to Tools > Options > Performance
Turn off hardware acceleration
Reduce undo history from 100 to 20 levels
Disable auto-save for non-critical files
Restart — usage should drop to about 50MB
conclusion: Took me way too long to figure this out. Hope it saves you the headache.
